checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains only text that is present in the source PDF. The section titles and image alt text are appropriate and derived from the source material. No hallucinated body text or fabricated sections were found.
There is no contact information provided in the source PDF, and none has been fabricated in the output JSON.

All three pages of the source PDF are accounted for in the JSON output. Section 1 corresponds to page 1, Section 2 to page 2, and Section 3 to page 3. All textual information from the slides is preserved.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no tables are missing or truncated in the output.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All substantial text content from the source PDF is present in the JSON. Text within callout boxes or map legends (e.g., 'QL 1 data collection completed' on page 1, or the county names and legend on pages 2 and 3) has been appropriately captured within the image alt text or the section elements, ensuring no information loss.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text transcription is highly accurate. Numbers, dates (e.g., '1/11-1/12'), and technical terms (e.g., 'NAD83(2011)') match the source exactly. Formatting such as the superscript in '4th' is correctly captured in the JSON structure.

checks.a_analysis: The output captures the content of all three pages without duplication. Each paragraph, heading, and list item corresponds to a unique element in the source document. While the section titles in the JSON often combine the text of the primary headings found within those sections, this is a structural metadata convention and does not constitute content duplication within the document body.

The document utilizes a logical heading hierarchy. Section 1 title matches the primary document title. Section 3 correctly identifies the two distinct headings ('2025 Data Collection Area' and 'North Carolina Collaboratory') as peer H2 elements. There are no skipped heading levels or incorrect nesting.
There are no tables present in the source document or the converted JSON output.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source pages. In Section 1, it captures the title, the map, and then the descriptive text blocks. In Section 3, it correctly processes the headings and the map before moving to the bulleted list on the right side of the page, maintaining the intended flow of information.

The bulleted list on Page 3 is correctly identified and structured as an unordered list in the JSON. Each of the 8 bullet points from the source is represented as a distinct list item. Formatting such as the superscript 'th' in the first section's text is also handled with high fidelity.
The source document contains no hyperlinks. No links were injected into the JSON output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output converts the multi-column and floating-element layout of the source PDF into a single-column vertical flow. While this differs from the PDF's presentation (e.g., text boxes that were to the right of the map on page 1 are now listed vertically), it is a standard and acceptable practice for responsive HTML conversion. The overall sequence of information follows the logical order of the pages.
The source document does not contain any tables; the information is presented as maps, lists, and text blocks.

The core visual elements of the document—the maps—are failed or missing. The map on Page 1 (showing LiDAR phases) is completely absent from the HTML output. The maps on Pages 2 and 3 are represented only by large solid green rectangles, which fails to convey any of the geographic or project data contained in the source maps. Additionally, the DPS and EMAP logos present in the source are missing from the HTML rendering.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains complex maps on every page: a phase map of North Carolina on page 1, and topographic refresh maps of Western NC on pages 2 and 3. It also includes DPS and EMAP logos in the header and footer areas.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows no image for the Page 1 map. For the maps on Pages 2 and 3, there are only solid green rectangular blocks. The DPS and EMAP logos are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document; specifically where maps should appear for each page section.

checks.a_analysis: The headings for the third section of the document are visually duplicated. The HTML output renders the page title and subtitle once as a combined heading and then immediately repeats them as two separate heading elements, creating redundant visual noise and organization issues.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 title: '2025 Data Collection Area' and subtitle 'North Carolina Collaboratory'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML shows three consecutive headings: '2025 Data Collection Area North Carolina Collaboratory', '2025 Data Collection Area', and 'North Carolina Collaboratory'.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML output, section corresponding to Page 3.
